skill.name: doc-coauthoring
skill.description: Guide users through a structured workflow for drafting, refining, and reader-testing documentation.
skill.summary: Coauthor technical docs, proposals, specs, and decision records through a guided three-stage workflow.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static shell execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and ordinary documentation prose. The skill has legitimate medium-risk behavior because it can use integrations to read shared context and can create or edit document files when the user approves the workflow.

medium_findings.title: Workspace Context Access Through Integrations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to use available integrations to fetch shared documents or read team channels. This is legitimate for documentation work, but it can expose sensitive workspace context if the user connects broad-access tools.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly instruct use of document and messaging integrations. The behavior is user-facing and expected, so the risk is exposure through authorized tools rather than malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Document File Creation and Editing
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to create markdown files and use replacement edits while drafting. This is expected behavior, but it can modify workspace files and should remain limited to user-approved document artifacts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool usage is explicit and repeated in the workflow. It is bounded to documentation drafting, but file writes are still a real capability.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The detected backtick patterns are Markdown inline code references to assistant tools, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands. No evidence of command execution was found in the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is Markdown workflow instruction text. There is no Ruby code, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain documentation workflow prose and references to Claude, audiences, templates, and feedback. No weak cryptographic algorithm, cryptographic API, or security-sensitive hashing behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of every cited line found no cryptographic code or algorithm names. The static detector appears to have matched unrelated prose.

low_findings.title: Static Network Reconnaissance Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The cited line asks the user to provide an unstructured information dump. It does not instruct port scanning, host discovery, network probing, or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain documentation workflow text. No network target, scanner, command, or probing activity is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Testing URL
low_findings.description: The skill points users to https://claude.ai for manual reader testing. This is transparent and relevant, but it sends users to an external service with their document content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicit and used for manual testing instructions. It is not hidden or malicious, but users should understand the destination.

content.user_title: Draft Better Documents With Guided Coauthoring
content.value_statement: Complex documents often fail because key context stays in the author's head. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through context gathering, section drafting, refinement, and reader testing.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through context gathering before drafting starts.
Builds document structure from a template, doc type, or user goals.
Drafts and refines sections through numbered options and targeted edits.
Uses files or artifacts to maintain a working document scaffold.
Tests documents with a fresh Claude or manual reader-testing process.
Checks final drafts for gaps, contradictions, redundancy, and unclear assumptions.
limitations: Quality depends on the context, files, and feedback the user provides.
Connected workspace tools may be required to read shared documents or channels.
The workflow is heavier than needed for short notes or simple copy edits.
The skill does not independently verify facts, links, or technical claims.

use_cases.title: Draft a Technical Spec
use_cases.description: Turn project context, constraints, and architecture notes into a structured technical specification.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Decision Document
use_cases.description: Capture trade-offs, alternatives, and rationale so stakeholders can understand a proposed decision.

use_cases.title: Improve Team Documentation
use_cases.description: Refine existing docs and test whether new readers can answer expected questions from the document.

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Doc
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the doc-coauthoring workflow to help me draft a document. Ask me the initial context questions first.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a proposal using the structured workflow. I will provide background, audience, constraints, and desired impact.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the refinement stage on my existing draft. Identify unclear sections, ask targeted questions, and suggest focused edits.

prompt_templates.title: Run Reader Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Test this document as a fresh reader. Predict reader questions, check ambiguity, and report what the document fails to explain.

output_examples.input: I need a decision doc for replacing our deployment process.
output_examples.output: A guided intake with audience, impact, constraints, and template questions, followed by a proposed section outline.

output_examples.input: Here is my draft spec. It feels unclear.
output_examples.output: A targeted review that identifies gaps, suggests section changes, and asks focused questions before editing.

output_examples.input: Reader test this proposal before I share it.
output_examples.output: A list of likely reader questions, ambiguity checks, and recommended fixes for sections that assume missing context.

best_practices: Provide audience, desired impact, constraints, and relevant background before asking for prose.
Use the numbered keep, remove, and combine feedback style during brainstorming.
Run reader testing before sharing important documents with stakeholders.
anti_patterns: Do not skip context gathering for documents that need stakeholder alignment.
Do not ask for a full polished draft before the structure is agreed.
Do not paste sensitive workspace content unless the connected tools and audience are appropriate.

faq.question: What document types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports technical specs, proposals, PRDs, decision docs, RFCs, and similar structured documents.

faq.question: Does it write the whole document at once?
faq.answer: No. It works section by section so the user can guide structure, content, and style.

faq.question: Can it use existing templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow asks for templates or example documents before creating the structure.

faq.question: Can it read Slack, Teams, or shared documents?
faq.answer: Only when appropriate integrations are available and the user directs the assistant to use them.

faq.question: What is reader testing?
faq.answer: Reader testing checks whether a fresh Claude or manual test reader can understand the document without hidden context.

faq.question: Is this best for quick copy edits?
faq.answer: No. It is best for substantial documents where structure, context, and reader understanding matter.
